article_meta.title: Once upon a time, school students would submit an essay, and teachers would mark it

raw_response: {"detections":[{"biasType":"Spin","biasedText":"an AI chatbot will interrogate them about it: put them on the spot in a two-way dialogue","explanation":"The word 'interrogate' and the phrase 'put them on the spot' are sensationalized, dramatic characterizations of what is described as a guided questioning process. These word choices inject a confrontational tone that goes beyond neutral description."},{"biasType":"Slant","biasedText":"But while AI is being rolled out at Australian schools in innovative ways, it is not coming to all of them, and not equally.","explanation":"The article focuses heavily on the benefits and innovative uses of AI in schools, and frames the main concern as unequal access rather than presenting substantive skepticism about AI in education. Teachers' caution about 'negative impacts on student wellbeing,' 'privacy issues,' and 'plagiarism' is condensed into a single paragraph and quickly moved past, while the bulk of the article promotes AI adoption."},{"biasType":"Subjective Qualifying Adjectives","biasedText":"brand-new tech","explanation":"'Brand-new' is a subjective qualifier that adds promotional emphasis. Not all the technologies mentioned (sensors, drones, coding) are necessarily 'brand-new.'"},{"biasType":"Opinion Statements Presented as Fact","biasedText":"It's not just about hammering in the lesson. It's also a way to ensure students do their own thinking, and haven't resorted to plagiarism or ChatGPT.","explanation":"The author presents as fact that this AI tool effectively 'ensures' students do their own thinking and haven't plagiarized, which is a contested claim about the tool's effectiveness presented without evidence or attribution."}]}

detections.biasedText: an AI chatbot will interrogate them about it: put them on the spot in a two-way dialogue
detections.explanation: The word 'interrogate' and the phrase 'put them on the spot' are sensationalized, dramatic characterizations of what is described as a guided questioning process. These word choices inject a confrontational tone that goes beyond neutral description.

detections.biasedText: But while AI is being rolled out at Australian schools in innovative ways, it is not coming to all of them, and not equally.
detections.explanation: The article focuses heavily on the benefits and innovative uses of AI in schools, and frames the main concern as unequal access rather than presenting substantive skepticism about AI in education. Teachers' caution about 'negative impacts on student wellbeing,' 'privacy issues,' and 'plagiarism' is condensed into a single paragraph and quickly moved past, while the bulk of the article promotes AI adoption.
'Brand-new' is a subjective qualifier that adds promotional emphasis. Not all the technologies mentioned (sensors, drones, coding) are necessarily 'brand-new.'

detections.biasedText: It's not just about hammering in the lesson. It's also a way to ensure students do their own thinking, and haven't resorted to plagiarism or ChatGPT.
detections.explanation: The author presents as fact that this AI tool effectively 'ensures' students do their own thinking and haven't plagiarized, which is a contested claim about the tool's effectiveness presented without evidence or attribution.
